subject: [PATCH v3 2/4] sunrpc: hardcode pool_mode to pernode, remove other modes

2026-06-29.patch_summary: This patch hardcodes the pool mode to pernode, removing other modes such as SVC_POOL_AUTO/GLOBAL/PERCPU. The default behavior on multi-NUMA hosts is changed to create one pool per NUMA node by default. This change simplifies the code and improves performance by providing proper thread affinity and NUMA-local memory allocation. The module parameter and netlink interfaces are preserved for backward compatibility, but writing any value other than 'pernode' will emit a deprecation notice.

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the removal of prefix matching in __param_set_pool_mode() causes previously accepted values like 'globalx' to be rejected with -EINVAL, and requested a fix to ensure backwards compatibility

Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that the kdoc modification in Documentation/admin-guide/kernel-parameters.txt is inconsistent, as it still refers to 'cpus to pools' despite percpu mode being removed and the patch correcting struct field comments to reflect a node-to-pool mapping.

Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that an unpooled svc_new_thread() running concurrently with an nfsd start or stop may observe m->count != 0 while pool_to is still NULL or already freed, and dereference it in svc_pool_map_put(), requesting the lock be dropped before calling try_to_unmap().

The reviewer noted that the patch introduces a potential dereference of pool_to[] when m->count is non-zero, and svc_new_thread() calls svc_pool_map_get_node(pool->sp_id) without holding any necessary locks or references.

Reviewer noted that pool_to[pidx] is dereferenced before the m->count == 0 check, making the WARN/return in svc_pool_map_get() dead code due to the sv_nrpools > 1 gate at the call site.
reviews.raw_body:
sysfs_match_string() compares the whole string, whereas the old
__param_set_pool_mode() matched prefixes (strncmp(val, "global", 6)
and friends). An input like "globalx" that the old code accepted is
now rejected with -EINVAL.

The commit message says:

  Writing any previously-accepted value succeeds silently

The four documented names still succeed, including with a trailing
newline since sysfs_streq() tolerates one, so only prefix-extended
strings change.

---

Regarding the kdoc modification above: with percpu removed the
map is node-to-pool; "cpus to pools" now contradicts the struct
field comments this same patch corrected ("maps pool id to
node" / "maps node to pool id").

---

svc_pool_map_put() frees pool_to[] and sets it back to NULL when the
last pooled reference drops.

Can an unpooled svc_new_thread() running concurrently with an nfsd
start or stop observe m->count != 0 while pool_to is still NULL (the
first get) or already freed (the last put), and dereference it here?
The reads of m->count and m->pool_to are not under the mutex.

The old default global mode returned numa_mem_id() in this case and
never touched pool_to[]; with pernode as the default this path runs
for every server. Would it help to consult pool_to[] only when the
service holds a reference, the way svc_pool_map_set_cpumask() is
already gated in svc_new_thread()?

        if (serv->sv_nrpools > 1)
                svc_pool_map_set_cpumask(task, pool->sp_id);

An unpooled service (sv_nrpools == 1) could then take numa_mem_id()
without reading the shared map.

---

This path now dereferences pool_to[] whenever m->count is non-zero.

svc_new_thread() calls it for every service, pooled or not, holding
neither svc_pool_map_mutex nor a map reference:

        node = svc_pool_map_get_node(pool->sp_id);

Unpooled services reach this too: lockd and the NFS callback are both
created with svc_create(), which passes npools = 1 and never calls
svc_pool_map_get(), so their m->count is whatever a pooled nfsd has
left set.

svc_pool_map_get() bumps the count before pool_to[] is allocated, and
the whole sequence is serialized only against other map writers:

        mutex_lock(&svc_pool_map_mutex);
        if (m->count++) {
                ...
        }
        npools = svc_pool_map_init_pernode(m);   /* allocates pool_to */

---

pool_to[pidx] is dereferenced before the m->count == 0 check, so if
count were ever 0 the NULL deref has already happened and the
WARN/return can never fire. The sv_nrpools > 1 gate at the call site
keeps this safe, so the guard is now dead code.

For the series as a whole, let's consider moving 5/4 to the front.
It looks like a fix that might want to be backported.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch hardcodes the pool mode to pernode, which is considered the right choice everywhere. It removes other modes such as SVC_POOL_AUTO/GLOBAL/PERCPU/PERNODE and simplifies pool map functions. The default behavior on multi-NUMA hosts changes to one pool per NUMA node by default. The module parameter and netlink interfaces are preserved for backward compatibility, but writing any previously-accepted value succeeds silently and reading always returns "pernode". The patch also updates the documentation to mark the pool_mode parameter deprecated and describe the new behavior.
